codeReview.summary: Solid, well-structured implementation covering all feature requirements. The quest generation flow is complete with API route (POST /api/quest/generate), Claude mock layer, Zod validation, XSS sanitization, DB persistence, and a client-side UI with form validation, loading/error states, and retry with input preservation. 37 tests cover auth, validation, XSS, AI integration, timeout, UI rendering, and accessibility. Two non-blocking observations noted.

issues.description: Sanitization is applied before Zod validation. If sanitizeInput strips enough characters to bring the string below the min-length threshold (e.g., user types '<script>abc</script>dream12' — 30 chars raw, but only 'abcdream12' = 10 chars after sanitization), the user gets a cryptic Zod validation error ('Dream must be at least 10 characters') rather than an XSS-related message. This is edge-case behavior and technically correct (we want to validate sanitized input), but the UX could be confusing. Acceptable for MVP.

Client-side validation uses value.trim().length for min-length check but value.length (untrimmed) for max-length check. This asymmetry means a user could type 500 spaces and it would pass the max-length check but be trimmed to 0 chars on submit. However, the server-side Zod validation catches this (min 10 chars on trimmed text) and the client-side empty check also catches pure-whitespace input, so this is not exploitable — just an inconsistency.
The client sends dream.trim() and localContext.trim() to the API, but the server also applies sanitizeInput() which calls .trim() again. Redundant but harmless.

Architecture document (architecture.md §6) lists the Claude client as 'src/lib/ai/anthropic.ts' but the actual file is 'src/lib/ai/claude.ts'. This is a documentation drift issue — the file naming works fine functionally. The architecture doc should be updated to reflect the actual filename.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture document (§6 AI Integration table) references 'src/lib/ai/anthropic.ts' as the Claude client, but the actual file is 'src/lib/ai/claude.ts'. This naming divergence could confuse future workers who reference the architecture doc.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md §6 table says 'src/lib/ai/anthropic.ts | Claude | Quest generation' but the actual file is src/lib/ai/claude.ts. This was established by the story-analysis feature (commit 1893519) before this quest-generation feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The quest/new page lives at src/app/[locale]/quest/new/ (outside the (auth) route group) but is still protected by middleware. The architecture document says protected routes live under src/app/[locale]/(auth)/. This is functionally fine since the middleware protects all non-public paths regardless of route groups, but it diverges from the documented convention.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: architecture.md §3 says 'src/app/[locale]/(auth)/ — route group that requires a valid session' and lists quest under (auth), but actual quest pages are at src/app/[locale]/quest/ without the (auth) group. Middleware at src/proxy.ts protects all non-public paths regardless, so security is not affected.

summary: The quest-generation-flow feature passes review. All 14 expected behaviors from the feature spec are implemented: dream/context inputs with min/max validation, empty/too-short/too-long error messages, XSS sanitization, loading state with encouraging text, error handling with retry that preserves inputs, timeout handling, 7-day mission plan generation via Claude mock, and DB persistence with Day 1 available / Days 2-7 locked. The implementation includes 37 well-structured tests (15 API route, 7 mock validation, 15 UI component) covering auth, validation, XSS, AI failure, timeout, loading state, retry, and accessibility. Two non-blocking issues noted: sanitization-before-validation ordering edge case and a trim asymmetry in client validation. Two shared-state observations flagged: architecture doc references wrong filename for Claude client (anthropic.ts vs claude.ts) and quest pages live outside the documented (auth) route group convention.
